definition: I2S splices exact XML keyword or token literals (xmlns, xml:id, DOCTYPE, SYSTEM, ID, CDATA markers, entity names, namespace tokens) into input byte positions, satisfying literal-equality parser checks that enable namespace registration, DTD construction, or automaton traversal.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace records each character comparand from the per-byte xmlns equality chain and splices the full 'xmlns\0' sequence into the input at the matching offset; value_profile's Hamming gradient provides no useful signal past the matched prefix for this discrete 6-way chain.
signature.one_line: I2S splices 'xmlns\0' into attribute name defeating per-byte equality chain

analysis.summary_one_line: An attribute name spelling exactly the six-byte literal 'xmlns\0' so the default-namespace gate at SAX2.c:1171 takes its true side.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An XML attribute whose split name resolves to the exact byte sequence name[0]='x', name[1]='m', name[2]='l', name[3]='n', name[4]='s', name[5]=0 (the literal 'xmlns' with no prefix and ns==NULL), satisfying the full six-character equality chain at SAX2.c:1170-1171.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the literal 'xmlns=' token in their bytes (the ASCII run 'xmlns' is the program-side target of the gate) and value_profile_cmplog can splice the exact 5-char/NUL-terminated comparand into the right input offset via I2SRandReplace; the winner reaches the false/resolving side at line 1160 and 1333 ([W] tags) and resolves 8/10 trials.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: value_profile only has havoc plus a CMP_MAP distance gradient; for a 6-way single-byte equality chain that terminates on a NUL, the prefix-distance bucket gives no useful gradient past the matched prefix, so the loser blocks 8/10 trials and only reaches the gate's true side once.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile: cmplog records the per-character comparand bytes ('x','m','l','n','s',0) at the SAX2.c:1170-1171 CMPs and substitutes them into the matching input offset, planting the exact 'xmlns\0' name that value_profile's Hamming/prefix gradient alone cannot synthesize.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is a six-byte exact-literal equality chain spelling 'xmlns' terminated by NUL.
The gate's first half checks name[0..2] equal 'x','m','l' with ns==NULL and non-HTML context.
The decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog beating value_profile with an I2S technique delta.
The winner resolves 8/10 trials while the loser blocks 8 and resolves only 1.
Winner-resolving seeds carry the literal 'xmlns' / 'xml' ASCII run the gate compares against.
value_profile has no I2SRandReplace mutator, only havoc plus token stack.
value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of the havoc and token stack.
value_profile only exposes a coarse prefix/Hamming distance bucket, not exact substitution.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the same 6-byte 'xmlns\0' equality-chain gate where value_profile resolves it as fast as value_profile_cmplog, showing the CMP_MAP prefix gradient alone suffices and I2S substitution is not the discriminator.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The winner seeds show 'xml' / 'xmlns=' bytes but the exact name[]='xmlns\0' value reaching the gate depends on xmlSplitQName output, which the seed-byte view does not directly confirm.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads the per-byte xmlns comparands from the CmpLogObserver and substitutes them into the ns prefix bytes, enabling cmplog to construct a prefix byte-distinct from 'xmlns' that takes the false side and reaches the undefined-namespace-prefix error path naive's havoc never finds.
signature.one_line: I2S reads xmlns comparands to route attribute prefix past false side

analysis.summary_one_line: A prefixed attribute whose namespace prefix is NOT the literal 'xmlns' (e.g. 'x', 'xlink'), so the 6-byte ns=='xmlns' gate falls false.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An attribute carrying a non-NULL namespace prefix (ns != NULL) whose first six bytes do NOT spell 'x','m','l','n','s',0 — i.e. a prefixed attribute like 'x:ink' or 'xlink:...' rather than a literal 'xmlns:' namespace declaration. Taking the FALSE side of the ns[0..5]=='xmlns' gate at line 1235-1236 routes execution past line 1303 (ns != NULL search) into the winner-only block.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving cmplog seeds contain prefixed-attribute tokens whose prefix is not 'xmlns': seed f32dee7a138d773b carries 'x:ink' and 'xmlns(xlink' near offset 0x0b-0x1e. At line 1236 the winner takes the FALSE side (W(T/F): T=0 F=6) and then enters the winner-only branch where xmlSearchNs / the undefined-namespace-prefix error path runs (lines 1306-1309 marked [W]).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking naive seeds instead supply a prefix that satisfies the equality, so at line 1236 the loser takes the TRUE side (L(T/F): T=5 F=0) and is consumed entirely inside the 'standard namespace definition' block (lines 1237-1301 marked [L]), never falling through to the winner-only code at line 1303+.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer logged by the byte-wise comparisons against the 'xmlns' literal (ns[0]=='x' ... ns[5]==0) and substitutes the captured operand bytes into the input at the matching offsets, letting cmplog construct an attribute prefix that is byte-distinct from 'xmlns' yet structurally a valid prefixed attribute — solving the multi-byte string roadblock that naive's blind havoc rarely lands on.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the 6-byte equality test ns[0..5]=='x','m','l','n','s',0 inside xmlSAX2AttributeInternal, and the globally blocked side is the false branch.
The blocked side that the winner must reach is the false branch.
At line 1236 the winner takes the false side six times while the loser takes the true side five times.
The winner-only code reached after the false branch is the xmlSearchNs / undefined-namespace-prefix error path at lines 1306-1309.
The loser executes the standard-namespace-definition block (true side, lines 1237-1301) and does not fall through.
A winner-resolving cmplog seed contains a non-'xmlns' prefixed-attribute token 'x:ink'.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes one logged CMP operand at offsets where the other operand is found in the input.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S; its mutators are havoc + token only.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the 6-byte ns=='xmlns' equality gate where cmplog and naive resolve the false (non-'xmlns' prefix) branch at statistically indistinguishable rates — i.e. naive reaches the prefixed-attribute path as fast as cmplog despite no I2S substitution.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff offsets are scattered/PARTIAL rather than a single concentrated run, so the exact input offset where I2S substitutes the prefix bytes is inferred from the seed token 'x:ink' rather than pinned by a clean per-offset W-vs-L concentration.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace logs the constant comparand 'xml:id' from the xmlStrEqual CMP and splices it directly onto the input bytes feeding fullname, instantly satisfying the 6-byte literal equality that value_profile's havoc/CMP_MAP stack cannot reliably construct.
signature.one_line: I2S splices 'xml:id' literal onto fullname bytes to satisfy equality

analysis.summary_one_line: An attribute whose full name equals the literal string 'xml:id' must appear in the parsed XML input.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The input must carry an attribute fullname byte-equal to the 6-byte literal "xml:id" so that xmlStrEqual(fullname, BAD_CAST "xml:id") returns true at SAX2.c:1415.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winning vpc seeds embed the literal 'xml:' prefix bytes (78 6d 6c 3a) inside a doctype/element region and vpc's I2SRandReplace can splice the logged 'xml:id' comparand operand directly onto the input bytes feeding fullname, producing the exact match; lineage shows an I2S-floor (mutation_op = -) ancestor.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace; its loser seeds carry unrelated attribute names like 'xlink:type' and never assemble the exact 6-byte 'xml:id' fullname, so the branch stays on the false side (resolved=0/10).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog's I2SRandReplace logs the constant comparand 'xml:id' from the xmlStrEqual CMP and substitutes it onto matching input bytes, instantly satisfying the literal equality that value_profile's havoc/CMP_MAP-only stack cannot reliably construct byte-by-byte.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is an exact string-equality test against the literal "xml:id".
The winner value_profile_cmplog resolves the true side in all trials while loser value_profile never does.
The loser value_profile is blocked in every trial.
Winning seeds embed the literal 'xml:' prefix bytes feeding the fullname comparison.
Loser seeds carry unrelated attribute names (xlink:type) rather than 'xml:id'.
value_profile_cmplog provides the I2SRandReplace input-to-state mutator that the loser lacks.
value_profile has no I2SRandReplace mutator.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on xmlStrEqual against a literal 'xml:id' in which value_profile (no I2S) resolves the true branch as often as value_profile_cmplog, showing the exact-literal gate is solvable without I2S substitution.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The winning seeds show 'xml:' bytes but the visible hex windows do not display a fully contiguous 'xml:id' fullname, so the exact-match assembly is inferred from source plus the lineage dash row rather than directly read off seed bytes.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace intercepts each byte comparison in the UTF-8 BOM conjunctive gate and substitutes 0xEF/0xBB/0xBF at matching input offsets; naive must blindly land the exact 3-byte magic and stalls blocked 8/10 trials.
signature.one_line: I2S substitutes UTF-8 BOM magic bytes into input start for conjunctive gate

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must begin with the exact 3-byte UTF-8 BOM 0xEF 0xBB 0xBF to take the true branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The first three bytes of the input must equal the literal magic sequence 0xEF 0xBB 0xBF (the UTF-8 byte-order mark) so that the conjunctive equality gate in[0]==0xEF && in[1]==0xBB && in[2]==0xBF evaluates true.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: cmplog intercepts each byte comparison (in[i]==0xEF etc.) into its CmpLogObserver and I2SRandReplace substitutes the recorded operand into the input; cmplog resolves 10/10 and the winning seeds carry the 0xEF byte (e.g. seed 5 starts f0 9f and contains ef at offset 0x09), and cmplog ancestor chains contain I2S-floor dash rows.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive has only havoc/token mutators with no CMP introspection, so it must blindly land the exact triple 0xEF 0xBB 0xBF; it stalls (blocked 8/10, 21.40h, hitcount 2), with the loser-blocking seeds never holding the BOM prefix at offsets 0-2.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the recorded comparison operand (0xEF/0xBB/0xBF) at matching input offsets, defeating the conjunctive magic-byte gate that naive's blind havoc cannot satisfy.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is a conjunctive equality test on the first three input bytes against the UTF-8 BOM 0xEF 0xBB 0xBF.
The true side (blocked side T) is the globally blocked side of this branch.
cmplog resolves the branch 10/10 while naive blocks 8/10 with long stall duration and low hitcount.
A winning cmplog seed contains the 0xEF byte that the BOM gate requires.
naive has only havoc and token mutators and no I2S capability.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which scans the input for an operand and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ngram4 resolves 9/10 versus naive's 2/10 via n-gram coverage feedback.
naive_ngram4 folds a rolling history of the last 4 edge IDs into the map index so a slot encodes a length-4 edge path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
an `NgramHook`
(`HookableInProcessExecutor`) that folds a rolling
history of the last 4 edge IDs into the map index. A map slot
therefore encodes a length-4 edge path (an n-gram of N=4) rather than
a single edge.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ngram4 has no I2S and no CMP_MAP, distinguishing its mechanism from cmplog.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: naive's havoc + token stack. No I2S, no CMP_MAP.

evidence_trail.claim: The loser side (false branch of L987) was taken 39 times by the loser seed set, confirming naive blocks at this gate.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the same 3-byte conjunctive magic-byte gate where cmplog fails to resolve the true branch faster than naive (no cmplog advantage), which would refute the I2S-substitution mechanism for this gate.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The naive_ngram4 hypothesis attributes its win to n-gram path retention, but the prompt offers no direct seed-byte evidence that ngram4 specifically retained BOM-adjacent inputs, only the aggregate resolve rate.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer for the switch dispatch on name[0] and splices the logged comparand ('l' / 'lt') into the entity-name input offset; naive and value_profile lack this and always fall through to default, never hitting the 'l' case.
signature.one_line: I2S plants 'l' into entity name first byte to hit switch case

analysis.summary_one_line: Entity-name token whose first byte is 'l' (matching the 'lt' predefined-entity literal) at the parsed name offset

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An XML entity reference whose parsed name begins with the byte 'l' (0x6c), reaching `case 'l':` in the switch on name[0], ideally spelling the predefined entity name 'lt'. The byte that selects the case is the first character of the entity name string passed to xmlGetPredefinedEntity.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving cmplog seed e987102bebde5d16 carries well-formed XML scaffolding (`<?xml version="1`, `<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM`) so a parsed entity name reaches xmlGetPredefinedEntity, and the cmplog/vpc I2S stage can substitute the logged switch/strcmp operand to land an 'l'-prefixed name; the true-side count rises (W hits=60 on line 299) where the loser never takes it.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking naive/value_profile seeds reach xmlGetPredefinedEntity far more often (L hits=390) but always fall through name[0] to `default:` (line 317 T=390 F=0) — they supply entity-name first bytes that never equal 'l', so the 'l' case (line 299) is T=60 for W but T=0 for L.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog and value_profile_cmplog run I2SRandReplace, which reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer for the switch/xmlStrEqual comparison on the entity name and splices the logged comparand ('l' / 'lt') into the input bytes at the matching offset; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must hit the exact name byte by chance, so they stall at default. value_profile's distance-bucket comparison feedback does not help here because a string-equality entity-name match is a discrete switch-case hit rather than a smooth distance signal — confirmed by value_profile losing identically to naive.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is `case 'l':` in a switch on name[0] inside xmlGetPredefinedEntity, whose true side is globally blocked.
The blocker line is reached via a switch on the first byte of the entity name.
Taking the 'l' case leads directly to an exact string-equality test against the literal "lt".
The winning cmplog seed contains well-formed XML scaffolding so a parsed entity name can reach xmlGetPredefinedEntity.
Winner takes the 'l' case while loser always falls through to default.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 299  T=60 F=0  T=0 F=390  case 'l':  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Loser reaches xmlGetPredefinedEntity far more often than the winner yet never takes the 'l' branch, so missing the matching name byte (not reachability) is what blocks it.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 317  T=0 F=60  T=390 F=0  default:

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which substitutes a logged CMP operand into the input at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: naive lacks I2S and value_profile also lacks I2SRandReplace, so neither can splice the matching entity name byte.
Both decisive pairs differ from their losers by exactly the I2S technique.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on a single-byte switch followed by an exact strcmp against a literal entity name showing naive resolving the 'l'/'lt' branch as fast as cmplog (no I2S advantage), or value_profile resolving it markedly faster than naive (implying value_profile's distance-bucket feedback, not I2S substitution, is what carries the winner).

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: Whether the winner's advantage comes from matching the full 'lt' string (xmlStrEqual) or merely the single 'l' switch byte — the branch instrumented is the switch case, so the deeper xmlStrEqual literal may be incidental rather than the gated comparand.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer for the switch dispatch on name[0] and xmlStrEqual comparisons against 'amp'/'apos', then substitutes the logged first-byte 'a' (and full entity name) into input bytes, routing through case 'a' that naive's blind havoc never reaches.
signature.one_line: I2S plants 'a' into entity name first byte to hit switch case

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must drive an entity-reference name whose first byte is 'a' (e.g. & / ') into xmlGetPredefinedEntity's switch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A parsed entity-reference name (passed as `name` to xmlGetPredefinedEntity) whose first byte name[0] equals 'a' (0x61), so the switch(name[0]) selects `case 'a':`; concretely the predefined entity names & or ' reaching the function via xmlSAX2GetEntity.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The cmplog winner repeatedly reaches and resolves the 'a'-case: it takes the true side 63 times (W T=63 F=0 at line 307) and the enclosing function shows hitcount 130 on the branch for the winner, indicating the entity-name dispatch lands on 'a' and exercises the amp/apos xmlStrEqual comparisons (lines 308/310 hit only on the W side).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser never selects `case 'a':` (L T=0 F=479 at line 307); every one of its 479 invocations falls through to `default:` (L T=479 F=0 at line 317), so name[0] is never 'a' and the amp/apos comparison lines 308/310 are never reached (T=0 F=0 for the loser).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer, which captures the operands of the switch dispatch on name[0] and the xmlStrEqual(name, "amp"/"apos") string comparisons, then substitutes the logged constant operand ('a', "amp", "apos") into the input bytes at the offsets where the current name appears, manufacturing entity names that route through `case 'a':`; naive has only blind havoc/token mutation and no CMP introspection, so it must stumble onto the exact first byte by chance and almost never does.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the `case 'a':` arm of a switch on name[0] in xmlGetPredefinedEntity, whose true side is globally blocked.
The dispatch is a switch on the first byte of the entity name.
Taking case 'a' leads to the amp/apos predefined-entity string comparisons.
The winner takes the 'a' case 63 times while the loser never does, falling through to default on all 479 calls.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 307  T=63 F=0  T=0 F=479  case 'a':  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The loser routes every invocation to the default arm, confirming name[0] is never 'a' for the loser.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 317  T=0 F=63  T=479 F=0  default:

evidence_trail.claim: The winner hits the branch far more often than the loser (130 vs 3).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=130  loser=3

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a logged CMP operand into the input at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S; only blind havoc/token mutation.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with switch(name[0]){case 'a': ...} gated behind an entity-name string comparison would be refuted if naive resolves `case 'a':` as often as cmplog (no significant cmplog>naive divergence), showing the first-byte/string dispatch is not what I2S substitution is solving.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff section shows broad whole-prefix divergence rather than a clean concentrated 'amp'/'apos' substring at a single offset, so the exact input offset where I2S writes the 'a'/amp/apos operand is inferred from source dispatch rather than read directly from the diff.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace repairs multi-byte structural literals (DOCTYPE, SYSTEM, entity names) that gate deeper DTD parsing by substituting CMP operands back into input offsets; this keeps the parse alive long enough for a distinct (name,name2,name3) triple to reach xmlHashAddEntry3.
signature.one_line: I2S repairs structural DTD literals so distinct hash triple is inserted

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must drive deeper DTD/entity parsing so the dict-backed hash table accumulates a distinct (name,name2,name3) triple, reaching line 580's false branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed DOCTYPE/DTD payload that parses far enough to insert a genuinely new (name,name2,name3) hash triple via xmlHashAddEntry3 on the dict path (line 571 true), so line 580 takes the false (non-duplicate) side. The structural literals (DOCTYPE, SYSTEM, element/entity names) must survive intact through the parser.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The cmplog winner seed keeps the structural literals intact (e.g. '<?xml version="1', '<!DOCTYPE ', 'a SYSTEM "dtds/1') and reaches xmlHashAddEntry3 with hitcount 2 on the branch; cmplog's I2SRandReplace can repair byte sequences that must match comparison operands deeper in the parse path, letting more declarations parse and populate the hash table.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Naive resolves only 2/10 and stays blocked 8/10 with hitcount 0 on the branch; without I2S substitution it relies on random havoc to keep the multi-byte structural tokens that gate deeper parsing, so the parse aborts before a distinct triple is inserted.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes one CMP operand into input offsets matching the other, repairing the byte literals that gate deeper DTD parsing; the lineage of the winning seed shows I2S-floor dash rows confirming an I2S contribution.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false side of the dict-path triple-pointer comparison at hash.c:580 in xmlHashAddEntry3.
The blocked side is the false branch, globally blocked.
The branch is on the dict path (line 571 true).
cmplog wins over naive at this branch via the I2S delta.
cmplog resolves 8/10 with branch hitcount 2 while naive stays at hitcount 0.
The cmplog winner seed keeps the DTD structural literals intact.
cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutes a logged CMP operand into matching input offsets.

scans the input for byte sequences matching one

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile wins over naive via the value_profile delta.
value_profile resolves 9/10 and blocks only 1/10.
value_profile preserves inputs whose CMP operands are closer to matching via CMP_MAP buckets.

An input that produces a

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx wins over naive via the ctx_coverage delta.
naive_ctx resolves 9/10 and blocks 0/10.
naive_ctx folds the call context into the edge-map index so the same edge from different call paths is distinct coverage.

the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it

evidence_trail.claim: xmlHashAddEntry3 is reachable from multiple distinct callers (entity, attribute, relaxng paths).

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S and relies only on havoc/token mutation.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose only deep-parse gate is a multi-byte equality literal showing naive resolving the dict-path new-triple insertion as fast as cmplog/value_profile/naive_ctx (no per-technique divergence).

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The branch itself compares interned dict pointers, not input bytes, so the technique advantage is attributed to upstream parse-path gating rather than to solving this comparison directly, which the byte diff cannot confirm offset-by-offset.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads the CMP buffer, finds the interned name3 pointer operand's byte pre-image in the input, and substitutes the exact name3 literal bytes, satisfying the dict-interned pointer identity comparison.
signature.one_line: I2S splices interned name3 literal bytes to satisfy pointer-equality gate

analysis.summary_one_line: Resolving the name3 pointer-identity check requires a deeply-nested DTD that interns and re-looks-up the same name3 string so dict pointers alias.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The input must place the literal name3 byte sequence (e.g. an entity/element name from the DTD) at the offset the parser interns, so that after dict interning the stored entry->name3 and the queried name3 are the same pointer.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog runs I2SRandReplace, which reads the post-execution CMP buffer, finds where one comparison operand appears in the input, and substitutes the matching operand byte-for-byte, directly placing the required name3 literal (resolved 8/10).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: value_profile has the CMP_MAP gradient but no I2SRandReplace mutator, so it cannot copy the exact operand into the input and must rely on havoc luck, staying blocked 8/10.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: I2SRandReplace (present in vpc, absent in value_profile) substitutes the logged CMP operand into the input to satisfy the name comparison feeding the dict-interned name3 identity; this I2S delta is the only difference between the two fuzzers.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a pointer-identity comparison of name3 inside xmlHashLookup3 on the dict path.
The comparison sits on the dict-interned branch, where equality is pointer identity established by dict interning.
The branch is reached via deep DTD parsing (DOCTYPE / entity / attribute-list-decl call chain).
minimizer beats naive on the calibrated_energy delta, resolving 9/10 vs naive 0/10.
minimizer derives per-seed mutation energy from intrinsic calibration which naive lacks.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
the energy uses ONLY intrinsic calibration and is NOT weighted by how often
a region has been hit

evidence_trail.claim: naive runs the havoc+token stack in a plain StdMutationalStage with no I2S and no calibrated energy.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: fast is identical to minimizer except the FAST AFLFast power schedule.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore `fast`
vs `minimizer`, not vs naive.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads intercepted CMP operands for prolog/markup keyword comparisons and substitutes the exact literal bytes at matching input offsets, repairing the XML structural tokens that keep wellFormed==1 long enough to reach the entity-ref path.
signature.one_line: I2S repairs XML prolog literals to preserve wellFormed and reach entity-ref gate

analysis.summary_one_line: A well-formed XML document whose body contains an entity reference '&name;' to an undeclared entity, keeping ctxt->wellFormed==1 and ent==NULL at the blocker

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Byte-exact XML structural tokens (the '<?xml version="1.0"?>' prolog and '<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/...' markup) must appear unbroken at the document head so the parser keeps wellFormed==1 long enough to parse an entity reference whose name resolves to no declared entity (ent==NULL).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The cmplog winning seed begins with a clean '<?xml version="1' / '.0"?>' / '<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/1' run, exactly the literal tokens libxml2 compares against; this preserves well-formed parsing into xmlParseEntityRef where the true side is taken (W L7658 T=3).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser seeds carry corrupting prefixes (binary '=\x06\x00\x00\x00', '____wu', 'EME=') ahead of and inside the prolog, so the parser errors out early and the entity-ref path is reached far less often (L L7658 F=86, T=0 on the blocker side).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads intercepted CMP operands (the literal prolog/markup keyword comparisons) from the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the expected literal at the matching input offsets, repairing the structural tokens that naive havoc can only stumble onto by luck.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace splices logged comparand bytes for prolog and DTD structural keyword gates (<?xml, version, DOCTYPE, str_xmlns pointer) into input positions, clearing the exact-equality chain that lets the parser populate attsDefault and reach the xmlns-default true branch.

analysis.summary_one_line: A DTD that defaults an unprefixed xmlns attribute (ATTLIST default namespace decl), reached only after the start-tag and DTD parse fully.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed XML document with a DOCTYPE/DTD that declares an attribute defaulting an unprefixed 'xmlns' (default-namespace) attribute via ATTLIST, so the defaulting loop at L9626 produces an entry whose attname == ctxt->str_xmlns and aprefix == NULL, taking the true branch at L9633.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winner seed (value_profile_cmplog trial 3) carries a '<?xml version="1.0"?>' prolog followed by '<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/1' — a syntactically valid document with a DTD reference, so the parser advances past xmlParseStartTag2's attribute loop into the attsDefault loop and hits the xmlns-default arm 3 times (W T=3 at L9633).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The loser seeds never reach the true branch: one loser carries a structurally similar prolog+DOCTYPE but the run produces only the false side / earlier arms (L T=0 F=8 at L9633, and the aprefix==str_xmlns arm L9648 fires T=5 F=3 instead), while the other loser is malformed nested '<a>'/'<b>' tag soup with no DTD defaulting at all, so xmlParseStartTag2 stalls in the attribute scan and never builds an xmlns-default entry.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile's CMP_MAP feedback. The intervening literal comparisons in the prolog/DTD ('<?xml', 'version', DOCTYPE keyword, the str_xmlns dictionary pointer compare) are exact-equality gates; I2SRandReplace splices the logged comparand bytes directly into the input so the document parses far enough to populate attsDefault and reach the L9633 xmlns-default true branch. value_profile (loser) lacks I2SRandReplace and only gets prefix-distance gradient buckets, which do not synthesize the exact byte sequences needed to clear the structural gates.

Winner seeds carry xmlns / xlink namespace tokens that drive namespace-list construction.
Loser seeds corrupt the leading bytes so the structural parse never establishes the namespace list.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which scans the input for one logged CMP operand and substitutes the other at those offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S and must reach the structural literals by blind havoc/token mutation.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating the same oldNs-non-NULL branch behind a multi-byte XML literal where cmplog and naive resolve at statistically indistinguishable rates would refute the I2S attribution.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The byte diff shows W/L differing across nearly all offsets (frame shift from BytesDelete/Insert), so I cannot isolate one CMP operand offset that I2S specifically repaired.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace splices logged per-byte tag/namespace literal comparands from xmlParseStartTag2/xmlParseQName into the input, assembling an xmlns-bearing start tag with '/>' close that advances ctxt->nsNr above the saved nsNr.
signature.one_line: I2S assembles xmlns start tag with '/>' close advancing namespace stack count

analysis.summary_one_line: A well-formed namespaced empty element (e.g. <b xmlns:x ... />) so that nsNr exceeds the saved nsNr when the empty-element close pops namespaces

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input bytes must form a syntactically valid start tag that declares at least one namespace (xmlns/xmlns:prefix) AND closes as an empty element with the literal '/>' so xmlParseStartTag2 increments ctxt->nsNr above the saved nsNr and the empty-element close path at line 11687 evaluates ctxt->nsNr - nsNr > 0 as true.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed carries a coherent XML-declaration plus element prologue ('1.0"?>.<!', 'aml.version="1.0"?>.<!DOCTY') aligning the parser into START_TAG handling; the winner reaches line 11688 nsPop (marked [W]) and the '&' reference path (11765-11768 [W]), evidence it parses element content with namespaces and pops them on the empty-element close.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking seeds stall in DTD/declaration text ('EMENT b (CDATA)>', '<!ATTLIST b xmlns:xlin', '#FIXED', 'http://www...') and never construct a closed namespaced empty element; coverage shows the loser takes the false branch at 11687 (T=0 F=58 at L11687) and never reaches the nsPop at 11688, so nsNr - nsNr is 0.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile: it splices logged CMP operands (the per-byte tag/namespace literal comparands inside xmlParseStartTag2/xmlParseQName) directly into the input bytes, assembling the exact 'xmlns'-bearing start tag and '/>' close that value_profile's gradient feedback plus havoc could not synthesize, letting the winner cross the multi-byte structural gate.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the namespace-pop guard inside the empty-element close path of the start-tag handler.
The blocked side is the true branch and the empty-element close calls nsPop only when nsNr grew.
The winner reaches the empty-element check via the '/>' close path after parsing a start tag.
The winner-resolving seed contains an XML declaration and element-structure bytes consistent with reaching START_TAG parsing.
The loser-blocking seeds are dominated by DTD/ATTLIST declaration text and never form a closed namespaced empty element.
At the blocker line the loser only takes the false side (never grows nsNr) while the winner takes the true side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L11687  T=2 F=0  T=0 F=58  if (ctxt->nsNr - nsNr > 0)

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace; it relies on CMP_MAP gradient feedback and havoc only.
value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, the input-to-state substitution mutator that distinguishes it from value_profile.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If value_profile (no I2S) resolves the true side at comparable rate to value_profile_cmplog once given a corpus seed already containing a valid 'xmlns'-bearing element, the I2S-gate attribution would be wrong — the divergence would instead be a power/scheduling effect, not a comparison-solving one.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The winner-resolving evidence is a single vpc seed whose bytes are fragmentary; the chain from those bytes to an actual closed namespaced empty element is inferred from coverage marks ([W] at 11688) rather than a fully reconstructed <... xmlns ... /> string in the dump.
